title: COALESCE Function
content:
### COALESCE Function

COALESCE returns the first non-NULL value from a list of expressions. It is the standard SQL way to handle NULL values.

---

### Basic COALESCE Syntax

```sql
SELECT COALESCE(salary, 0) AS salary_or_zero
FROM employees;

-- Returns the salary value if not NULL, otherwise 0
```

### COALESCE with Multiple Arguments

```sql
-- Returns the first non-NULL value in order
SELECT COALESCE(preferred_name, first_name, 'Unknown') AS display_name
FROM employees;
```

### COALESCE vs ISNULL vs NVL

| Function | Database | Notes |
|----------|----------|-------|
| COALESCE(value, default) | SQL Standard | Accepts 2+ arguments |
| ISNULL(value, default) | SQL Server | Accepts exactly 2 arguments |
| NVL(value, default) | Oracle | Accepts exactly 2 arguments |
| IFNULL(value, default) | MySQL | Accepts exactly 2 arguments |

### Practical Examples

```sql
-- Calculate total compensation with fallback
SELECT name,
       COALESCE(salary, 0) + COALESCE(bonus, 0) AS total_pay
FROM employees;

-- Handle NULL in string concatenation
SELECT CONCAT(first_name, ' ', COALESCE(middle_name, ''), ' ', last_name) AS full_name
FROM employees;

-- Use in WHERE clause
SELECT * FROM employees
WHERE COALESCE(termination_date, '9999-12-31') > CURRENT_DATE;
```

> **Key Point:** COALESCE evaluates arguments left to right and stops at the first non-NULL value. All arguments must be of compatible data types.

quiz.question: How many arguments can COALESCE accept?
